channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: NYC mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani in second place in new poll
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – It is under four months away until voters hit the polls for the Democratic primary for New York City mayor. New polling from the Honan Strategy Group showed some interesting results about the race.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Trump and Zelenskyy through the years: From a 'perfect' call to an Oval Office meltdown
item.description: The first time Donald Trump spoke to Volodymyr Zelenskyy, he tried to pressure Ukraine’s new leader to dig up dirt on Joe Biden ahead of the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: California lawmaker revives AI whistleblower protections concerns in new bill
item.description: The California lawmaker who introduced an AI safety protection bill later vetoed by Gov. Gavin Newsom has again introduced legislation to regulate the powerful technology. Sen.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Texas GOP leaders defend appointee after tense exchange over diversity hiring
item.description: Several of Texas’ top Republicans are defending Gov. Greg Abbott’s pick to head the state’s water agency following pointed questions from a GOP lawmaker over the agency’s diversity hiring policies.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Zelenskyy leaves White House without signing minerals deal after Oval Office blowup
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump berated Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for being “disrespectful” Friday in an extraordinary Oval Office meeting, then abruptly called off the signing of a minerals deal that Trump said would have moved Ukraine closer to ending its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, ZEKE MILLER and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: European leaders support Ukraine amid tensions with Trump
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — European leaders pledged late Friday to stand by Ukraine in the wake of the contentious Oval Office meeting between U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Iowa governor signs bill removing transgender protections from civil rights code
item.description: A new bill signed by Iowa Gov. Kim Reynolds will remove gender identity protection from the state's civil rights code after it was added by Democratic legislators 18 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Leland Vittert’s War Notes: Showdown in the Oval
item.description: President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance’s double-teaming of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky in the Oval Office channeled an inner anger among common-sense Americans.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration approves major nearly $3 billion arms sale to Israel
item.description: The Trump administration has approved a major nearly $3 billion arms sale to Israel, bypassing a normal congressional review to provide the country with more of the 2,000-pound bombs that it has used in the war against Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: European leaders pledge to stand by Ukraine after confrontational Oval Office meeting with Trump
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — European leaders pledged late Friday to stand by Ukraine in the wake of the contentious Oval Office meeting between U.S.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO and SUSIE BLANN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: VP Vance shares special message to ailing Pope Francis amid breathing crisis
item.description: JD Vance shared that though he was “surprised" by Pope Francis’ criticism of President Trump’s immigration policy, he believes the pontiff is “fundamentally a person who cares about the flock of Christians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What US lawmakers are saying about the White House clash between Trump and Zelenskyy
item.description: Key Republicans and Democrats have been stalwart supporters of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Epstein files debacle spurs new interest in contents of promised RFK, MLK assassination files
item.description: In accordance with President Trump's executive order, the plan to release the MLK and RFK files is due on March 9, just weeks after the DOJ revealed highly anticipated Epstein files, many of which had already been released.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Children with disabilities swept up in DEI fight, advocates say
item.description: Children with disabilities are getting swept up in the Trump administration's crusade against diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) and its push to end the Education Department, advocates say.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Behold the beaver! Drive is on to make prehistoric giant Minnesota’s official fossil
item.description: Minnesota is among a handful of states that doesn’t have an official fossil among its symbols. The Minnesota Science Museum is hoping to change that.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Florida ups the stakes for crimes by immigrants in the US illegally
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — For most people in Florida, misdemeanor theft can result in up to a year in jail and a $1,000 fine. For an immigrant illegally in Florida, that same crime now carries a felony punishment of up to five years behind bars and a $5,000 fine.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB and KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge finds mass firings of federal probationary workers were likely unlawful
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge in San Francisco on Thursday found that the mass firings of probationary employees were likely unlawful, granting temporary relief to a coalition of labor unions and organizations that has sued to stop the Trump administration’s massive dismantling of the federal
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prince Harry's immigration status under fire over drugs, possible lies
item.description: Prince Harry's immigration status has come under fire after he admitted to recreational use of marijuana, cocaine and psychedelics.
item.dc:creator: Tom Palmer, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada Democrats push for more ballot drop boxes to avoid election delays
item.description: Nevada Democrats unveiled a proposal Thursday to increase the number of ballot drop boxes in the days before an election.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada's Cortez Masto slams Trump administration cuts amid bird flu response
item.description: Democratic Nevada Sen. Catherine Cortez Masto criticized the Trump administration Thursday, saying recent terminations are worsening the federal government’s response to the bird flu outbreak and the rise price of eggs.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate Republicans seek major changes to House-passed Trump budget bill
item.description: Senate Republicans are staring down a major fight to overhaul the House's budget resolution as lawmakers eye big changes. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.), with the help of President Trump’s muscle, was able to get the House's plan to enact Trump's sweeping legislative agenda past a key hurdle Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, UK's Keir Starmer speak after meetings
item.description: Starmer is expected to try to convince Trump that Ukraine and European leaders need a place at the table for peace talks.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump shifts date for Canada and Mexico tariffs
item.description: Little about how Trump discusses tariffs is normal — not only because he threatens tariffs on a weekly, even daily, basis, but also because it's often unclear if or when those tariffs will happen.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: Federal freeze on energy and climate funding causing concern, confusion in Minnesota
item.description: Some of the money has been released since federal judges ordered the funding to be unfrozen. But weatherization programs and rebates for home efficiency upgrades are still on hold. State and local officials say a lack of communication has led to confusion and uncertainty.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: DFL, GOP team up on bills to battle absenteeism in Minnesota schools
item.description: Minnesota lawmakers this week are moving forward on five bills aimed at addressing chronic absenteeism in Minnesota schools, and DFLers and Republicans are working together on policy solutions.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman, MPR News

item.title: Pro-Palestinian protesters occupy Barnard College, disrupting classes
item.description: According to Columbia University Apartheid Divest, the protesters demanded "the immediate reversal" of two student expulsions.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Rogers, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration says it's cutting 90% of USAID foreign aid contracts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration said it is eliminating more than 90% of the U.S. Agency for International Development’s foreign aid contracts and $60 billion in overall U.S. assistance around the world, putting numbers on its plans to eliminate the majority of U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, MARK SHERMAN and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Musk says federal workers should expect another round of ‘pulse check’ emails
item.description: President Trump warned federal workers who did not reply to recent emails asking them to describe “five things” they accomplished are “on the bubble” suggesting they are at risk of losing their jobs.
item.dc:creator: Heidi Glenn, MPR News

item.title: Lawmakers differ over location of Florida’s Black history museum
item.description: Florida has a rich and long history involving contributions from its Black residents. There are areas across the state highlighting those struggles and efforts. Some of those key moments are displayed at the Robert W Saunders, Sr Public Library in Tampa.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada Gov. Lombardo, AG Ford clash on immigration policies ahead of potential 2026 matchup
item.description: Republican Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo and Democratic Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford -- who could face off in the 2026 gubernatorial election -- swapped jabs in competing statements regarding guidance about immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy expected to sign minerals deal Friday: Trump
item.description: Details are vague but what is known is that the United States is requesting revenue from Ukraine's rare minerals.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: What Medicaid cuts could mean for Minnesota children
item.description: About one in five Minnesotans are on Medicaid. That shakes out to about one in six adults and three in 10 children.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Out to Lunch: Speaker Demuth doesn’t want to be defined by one part of her identity
item.description: Earlier this month, Republican Rep. Lisa Demuth became the first Black person and female Republican to hold the role in the Minnesota House of Representatives. Minnesota Now sat down with her in a new series called, “Out to Lunch.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration sets the stage for large-scale federal worker layoffs in new memo
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration says federal agencies should begin planning to eliminate employee positions. The process is known as a reduction in force. Agency leaders must submit their plans by March 13.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Judge tells Trump administration it has less than 2 days to resume USAID funding
item.description: U.S. District Judge Amir H. Ali said officials have provided no evidence of compliance with repeated orders to unfreeze the money.
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt, MPR News

item.title: Canada wants new oil pipelines to avoid Trump tariffs; nobody wants to build them
item.description: Canada sends 90 percent of oil exports to U.S. refiners. The Canadian government would have to play a significant role in any project to build new oil pipelines in Canada to overcome regulatory, financial and political hurdles and activist opposition, industry experts said.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: No savings expected from nearly 40% of DOGE-canceled contracts
item.description: Nearly 40% of the federal contracts that the Trump administration claims to have canceled as part of its signature cost-cutting program aren’t expected to save the government any money, the administration's own data shows.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY Associated Press, Fox 5
